title: Reference Tipper Axes From the Visu_MCP_Dual Screen
summary: Use the Visu_MCP_Dual tipper machine control panel to move axes to the home position with REF or automatically reference both tippers with AUTO REF when enabled, then verify referenced status by checking the green 'R' indicators for A1, Z1, A2, and Z2.
when_to_use: Use when tipper axes need to be moved to the home position or referenced from the Visu_MCP_Dual screen. The source states REF typically only has to be done when the system is powered on, and AUTO REF is available after the cycle has been reset.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as a complete troubleshooting procedure when referencing does not complete; the source does not provide additional troubleshooting steps.
Do not use AUTO REF unless the button is enabled; the source states it is enabled after the cycle has been reset (press RESET).

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform axis homing or referencing from the Visu_MCP_Dual screen and verify referenced status using the axis indicators.

safety_notes: An axis being out of reference may cause a tipper fault.
The source does not provide full safety boundaries or precondition detail for this action.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI

access_required: Access to the operator station HMI
system_preconditions: Use AUTO REF only when the button is enabled after the cycle has been reset (press RESET).

visual_references.description: Operator Station Control Panel on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen showing REF, AUTO REF, and other primary controls.
Operator station HMI screen artifact associated with manually referencing axes during maintenance procedures.
Operator station HMI artifact associated with manually referencing tipper axes during maintenance.
System screen artifact with nearby startup text referencing AUTO REF and homing axes.

steps.title: Open the Visu_MCP_Dual screen and identify REF and AUTO REF
steps.purpose: Locate the tipper machine control panel and confirm the reference controls are available on the correct HMI screen.
steps.instruction: Open the Tipper Machine Control Panel (Visu_MCP_Dual) screen and identify the REF and AUTO REF controls on the operator station control panel.
steps.expected_result: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen is open and the REF and AUTO REF controls are visible.
steps.healthy_condition: The primary tipper control screen is displayed and the relevant controls can be identified.
steps.failure_condition: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen cannot be accessed or the REF and AUTO REF controls cannot be identified.

actions.label: Open Visu_MCP_Dual
actions.exact_action: Open the Tipper Machine Control Panel ("Visu_MCP_Dual") screen and identify the REF and AUTO REF controls.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Locate the REF and AUTO REF buttons on the Operator Station Control Panel for the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The required HMI screen cannot be accessed.
The REF or AUTO REF controls are not visible on the screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Tipper Machine Control Panel ("Visu_MCP_Dual" screen) has buttons that control tipper movement and allows for fault reset. NOTE: This is the primary screen that station operators will need to use.
Figure 4-19 appears on page 47 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_4_1_2_1_tipper_machine_control_panel_visu_mcp_dual_screen.

steps.title: Use REF to move axes to the home position
steps.purpose: Move the axes to the home position using the documented REF control sequence.
steps.instruction: To move axes to the home position, select REF and press the "-" button to initiate movement.
steps.expected_result: The axes begin moving to the home position.
steps.healthy_condition: Axis movement is initiated after REF is selected and the "-" button is pressed.
steps.failure_condition: Axis movement does not start after using REF and the "-" button.

actions.label: Start REF homing
actions.exact_action: Select REF and press the "-" button to initiate movement.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the REF control on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen and identify the associated "-" button used to initiate movement.
Reference-related HMI layout associated with manually referencing axes.

stop_or_escalate_if: Axis movement does not initiate after selecting REF and pressing the "-" button.
Unexpected fault behavior is observed during or after the reference attempt.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The REF button moves the axes to the "home" position. Press the "-" button to initiate movement. This typically only has to be done when the system is powered on.

steps.title: Use AUTO REF when enabled to reference both tippers
steps.purpose: Automatically reference the axes of both tippers using the documented automatic reference function.
steps.instruction: If AUTO REF is enabled, use AUTO REF to automatically reference the axes of both tippers. The source states this button is enabled after the cycle has been reset (press RESET).
steps.expected_result: Automatic referencing of both tipper axes is initiated.
steps.healthy_condition: AUTO REF is enabled and starts the automatic reference process for both tippers.
steps.failure_condition: AUTO REF is not enabled when needed or automatic referencing does not complete.

actions.label: Start AUTO REF
actions.exact_action: Use AUTO REF when that button is enabled to automatically reference the axes of both tippers.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Locate the AUTO REF button on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen and confirm whether it is enabled.
Reference-related HMI screen associated with manual and automatic axis referencing context.
Startup-related screen context mentioning AUTO REF for homing axes.

stop_or_escalate_if: AUTO REF is required but the button is not enabled.
Automatic referencing does not complete after AUTO REF is used.
A tipper fault remains or appears after the reference attempt.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The AUTO REF button automatically references the axes of both tippers. This button is enabled after the cycle has been reset (press RESET).

steps.title: Check A1, Z1, A2, and Z2 reference indicators
steps.purpose: Verify reference status for each axis using the HMI display area.
steps.instruction: Verify axis reference status in the A1, Z1, A2, and Z2 display area by checking the 'R' indicator for each axis.
steps.expected_result: Each axis display shows an 'R' indicator that can be checked for referenced status.
steps.healthy_condition: The axis display area is visible and each axis has an 'R' indicator available for review.
steps.failure_condition: One or more axis indicators do not show referenced status or the display cannot be verified.

actions.label: Inspect axis reference indicators
actions.exact_action: Check the A1, Z1, A2, and Z2 display area and review the 'R' indicator for each axis.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Find the A1, Z1, A2, and Z2 display area on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Any axis does not show referenced status after the documented controls are used.
The axis status display cannot be interpreted from the HMI.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The "A1" and "Z1" boxes show the current position (in steps) on the A-axis and Z-axis of the left gripper. The "A2" and "Z2" boxes show the current position (in steps) on the A-axis and Z-axis of the right gripper. The 'R' denotes whether the axis has been referenced.

steps.title: Confirm the green 'R' status for referenced axes
steps.purpose: Confirm successful referencing using the documented visual status indicator.
steps.instruction: Confirm that the 'R' circle is green to indicate the axis has been referenced.
steps.expected_result: The 'R' indicator for referenced axes is green.
steps.healthy_condition: The 'R' circle is green for the axes that have been referenced.
steps.failure_condition: The 'R' circle is not green for one or more axes, indicating the axis is still out of reference.

actions.label: Confirm green R indicator
actions.exact_action: Confirm that the 'R' circle is green to indicate the axis has been referenced.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Review the axis status area and confirm the 'R' circle color for each axis.

stop_or_escalate_if: The 'R' circle does not turn green for one or more axes.
A tipper fault is present or occurs because an axis remains out of reference.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The 'R' in the cycle denotes whether or not the axis has been "referenced": if the circle is green, the axis has been referenced. An axis being "out of reference" may cause a tipper fault.

success_criteria: The axes are moved to home or referenced using REF or AUTO REF.
The corresponding 'R' indicators for A1, Z1, A2, and Z2 show green for referenced axes.
healthy_conditions: Visu_MCP_Dual is accessible and the REF/AUTO REF controls are visible.
REF initiates movement to the home position when used with the "-" button.
AUTO REF is enabled after RESET and starts automatic referencing of both tippers.
The 'R' indicator is green for referenced axes.
failure_conditions: REF does not initiate axis movement.
AUTO REF is not enabled when expected or does not complete referencing.
One or more axes remain out of reference.
A tipper fault occurs or remains because an axis is out of reference.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if axis reference status does not become indicated as referenced after the documented controls are used.
Escalate if AUTO REF is needed but not enabled after the cycle has been reset.
Escalate if a tipper fault is present or occurs due to an axis being out of reference.
Escalate if referencing does not complete, because the source does not provide additional troubleshooting.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary Visu_MCP_Dual control panel showing REF, AUTO REF, and axis status areas.
Reference-related HMI screen associated with manual axis referencing context.
Reference-related HMI screen associated with manual and automatic axis referencing context.
Startup-related context mentioning AUTO REF and homing axes.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The REF button moves the axes to the "home" position. Press the "-" button to initiate movement. This typically only has to be done when the system is powered on.
The AUTO REF button automatically references the axes of both tippers. This button is enabled after the cycle has been reset (press RESET).
The 'R' in the cycle denotes whether or not the axis has been "referenced": if the circle is green, the axis has been referenced. An axis being "out of reference" may cause a tipper fault.

missing_details: The source does not clearly assign this action to a normal operator role; L2_support is retained from the candidate.
The source does not provide a complete safety procedure or motion hazard controls for referencing.
The source does not specify whether production must be stopped before performing this procedure.
The source does not specify whether lockout/tagout is required.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source does not provide additional troubleshooting steps if referencing does not complete.
